body:
### Overview

Cyscale fails this control when an EKS cluster has no Kubernetes Secrets encryption configuration or the encryption provider key ARN is empty. Kubernetes Secrets should be envelope-encrypted with a customer-managed AWS KMS key.

For existing EKS clusters, enabling secret encryption is supported, but existing secrets must be rewritten to be encrypted with the new provider.
name: Ensure Kubernetes Secrets are encrypted using Customer Master Keys (CMKs) managed in AWS KMS
remediationDescription:
## Remediation

Enable EKS secrets encryption with a customer-managed KMS key.

1. Create or select a customer-managed symmetric KMS key in the same region as the cluster.
2. Ensure the EKS service principal and cluster role can use the key.
3. Associate the KMS key with the EKS cluster for Kubernetes Secrets encryption.
4. Rewrite existing Kubernetes Secrets so they are encrypted by the new provider.
5. Re-run sync and assessment.

AWS CLI example:

```bash
aws eks associate-encryption-config --cluster-name {{asset.name}} --encryption-config '[{"resources":["secrets"],"provider":{"keyArn":"<kms-key-arn>"}}]' --region {{asset.region}}
```

Rewrite existing secrets after encryption is enabled:

```bash
kubectl get secrets --all-namespaces -o json | kubectl replace -f -
```

Cyscale will pass when the EKS cluster has a non-empty secrets encryption provider key ARN.
description: Amazon EKS clusters that do not encrypt Kubernetes Secrets with an AWS KMS customer-managed key.
